name: resolve_1g_l3_ps_not_checked_in_l3
code:
proof fn phi_5_resolve_1g_l3_ps_not_checked_in_l3(pt: PageTable, l4i: L4Index, l3i: L3Index)
    requires
        pt.kernel_l4_end <= l4i < 512,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).is_Some(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr),
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.present,
        pt.l3_tables@[pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l4(l4i).unwrap().addr].value()[l3i].perm.ps == false,
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_1g_l3(l4i, l3i).is_Some(),
    ensures
        pt.spec_resolve_mapping_l3(l4i, l3i).is_Some(),
{
}
reason: When an L3 entry is present but NOT a 1G page (ps==false), both spec_resolve_mapping_1g_l3 and spec_resolve_mapping_l3 return Some — the 1G resolver does not filter out non-ps entries, so a 4K/2M entry could be misinterpreted as a 1G mapping candidate
